name: Internal Pluralism and the Limits of Pairwise Comparisons

description: arXiv:2607.02672v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Local pairwise comparisons are a standard tool for learning how people want decision rules to work, e.g., in participatory design or alignment. However, their use builds in two strong assumptions: that local comparisons are sufficient evidence about how a person wants an automated decision rule to behave, and that people can always answer thos...
